title: pseudognaphalium canescens subsp. microcephalum
intro: Pseudognaphalium canescens subsp. microcephalum, commonly known as a small-leaved white everlasting, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, and is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory problems, and has been found to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Pseudognaphalium canescens subsp. microcephalum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal remedies for its potential health benefits.
